name: NMH Credit Union
description_short: Skyline Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving Skyline employees and their families, offering savings, loans, and financial guidance at competitive rates.
description_long:
Skyline Credit Union is a state-chartered, member-owned credit union located in Tennessee (area code 615). It operates as a closed-charter institution, meaning membership is limited to employees of Skyline and their eligible family members. The credit union is governed by a volunteer board of directors elected annually by the membership, and each member holds one vote regardless of account balance—a hallmark of the cooperative model.

The credit union offers core financial products including savings accounts, loans, and financial guidance. Seasonal loan specials are offered throughout the year, providing members with periodic opportunities for favorable borrowing terms. Financial guidance is included as part of the member value proposition, positioning the CU as more than a transactional institution. All services are described as delivered at competitive rates in a safe and efficient manner.

What distinguishes Skyline Credit Union is its tight community focus. Membership is tied to a common employer bond, which keeps the institution small, personal, and mission-aligned. The credit union publishes a quarterly newsletter (CU Sidelines), demonstrating ongoing member communication. Staff is named and accessible—six employees serve members directly—and the board is composed of seven named volunteer directors, indicating strong governance transparency for an institution of this size.

Honestly, Skyline Credit Union's website provides very limited product detail. Specific loan types, interest rates, minimum deposits, digital banking capabilities, ATM access, or NCUA insurance status are not disclosed on the public site. Prospective members should call 615-345-3471 directly to understand the full product lineup. This CU is best suited for Skyline employees seeking a low-overhead, member-owned alternative to commercial banking—not for consumers seeking a full-featured financial institution with broad product breadth or digital-first services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Member-owned cooperative — each member has one vote regardless of account size
Closed-charter keeps the membership small and service personal
Volunteer board of seven named directors provides governance transparency
Seasonal loan specials offered throughout the year
Quarterly member newsletter (CU Sidelines) keeps members informed
Named, accessible staff — six employees listed publicly
Mission explicitly includes financial guidance alongside products
cons: Membership restricted to Skyline employees and family — general public cannot join
Website discloses almost no product detail: no rates, no loan types, no minimum balances
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or ATM network access
Very small institution — limited product breadth compared to larger banks or credit unions
best_for: Skyline employees wanting a member-owned alternative to commercial banking
Family members of Skyline employees seeking basic savings and loan products
Borrowers who want access to periodic loan specials from a low-overhead lender
diagnosis: Skyline Credit Union is best for current Skyline employees and their families who want a small, member-owned financial institution with a personal feel and periodic loan specials. The main caveat is severe information scarcity — the website does not disclose rates, specific loan products, or digital banking features, so prospective members must call to evaluate fit.
services: Member voting and annual membership meetings
